title: Mobile QA Test Scenario v2
description: Schema v2 for mobile QA test scenario files generated by /mobile-automator:generate. Supports dynamic waits, optional steps, conditional branching, nested sub-flows, variable capture/reuse, retry policies, and pattern-based assertions.

$schema_version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be '2.0' for v2 scenarios.

name.description: Human-readable name of the test scenario

description.description: What this scenario tests and what business value it validates

app_package.description: Bundle ID or package name of the app under test

metadata.description: Design-time metadata only. Execution-time data (device_model, api_level, timestamp) belongs in the result schema.

app_version.description: Build variant or version targeted by this scenario (e.g., 'staging-latest', '2.4.1-debug')

target_version.description: Optional: target app major version (e.g., 'v4.x') for scenario compatibility tracking

variables.description: Declare variables that will be captured during test execution and used in later steps or assertions.

type.description: Data type of the captured variable

description.description: Human-readable description of what this variable represents

preconditions.description: Required state and setup actions before test execution begins.

app_state.description: Required app state before the test begins

device_actions.description: Automated setup actions to perform on the device before the scenario starts

target_package.description: App package to target (for clear_app_data and uninstall_app)

value.description: Value parameter for the action (e.g., orientation value)

description.description: Human-readable description of why this action is needed

device_properties.description: Required device properties at test start

notes.description: Human-readable notes about preconditions that cannot be automated (e.g., backend requirements, test account setup)

steps.description: Ordered list of test steps to execute.

assertions.description: Verifications to run at specific points during the scenario.

id.description: Unique step identifier in snake_case (e.g., 'tap_login', 'wait_for_home'). Used for screenshot naming and assertion references.

action.description: The automation action to perform. New in v2: wait_for_element, wait_for_element_gone, wait_for_loading_complete, capture_value, clear_app_data, long_press, double_tap, scroll_to_element.

description.description: What this step does in plain language — should explain the intent, not just the mechanics

target.description: Semantic description of the element to interact with or wait for. Used by the AI to locate the element via mobile_list_elements_on_screen.

target_pattern.description: Optional: regex pattern to match against element text when the element cannot be described with a static string (e.g., '\\d+ points' for a dynamic points tab label)

value.description: Input value for the action: text to type, swipe direction (up/down/left/right), button name (HOME/BACK/ENTER), URL to open, or scroll direction

checkpoint.description: Whether to capture a reference screenshot after this step. Screenshots are saved as mobile-automator/screenshots/<scenario_id>/step_<id>.png

expected_state.description: Description of the expected visual state after this step completes — used by the executor for semantic validation

optional.description: If true, failure to find or interact with the target element is silently ignored. Execution continues to the next step. Use for UI elements that only appear sometimes (A/B tests, dialogs, promotions).

condition.description: When to execute this step. If the condition evaluates to false, the step is skipped.

type.description: The type of condition to evaluate

property.description: Device property name for device_property conditions (e.g., 'api_level', 'orientation', 'platform')

step_id.description: Step ID reference for previous_step_skipped conditions

element_description.description: Element to check for element_visible conditions

on_failure.description: What to do when this step fails. 'fail' = mark scenario failed (default). 'skip' = continue to next step silently. 'retry' = attempt retry_policy before failing.

retry_policy.description: Retry configuration. Only applicable when on_failure is 'retry'.

max_attempts.description: Total number of attempts including the first (e.g., 3 means 1 initial + 2 retries)

backoff_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait between retry attempts

wait_config.description: Configuration for wait-type actions (wait_for_element, wait_for_element_gone, wait_for_loading_complete).

type.description: The wait condition to poll for

indicator.description: For loading_complete: which type of loading indicator to wait for disappearance of

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait in milliseconds before failing the step

capture_to.description: Variable name to store the captured value in. Must match a variable declared in the root 'variables' block. Used with capture_value action.

sub_steps.description: Nested sub-flow to execute when this step's condition is met. After all sub-steps complete, execution resumes at the next top-level step. Use for NESTED_CONDITIONAL patterns where an entire branching sub-flow must run under a specific condition.

after_step.description: Step ID (by name) after which this assertion runs. Must reference a valid step id.

type.description: Assertion type. New in v2: pattern_match, value_matches_variable, element_count, visual_state, text_changed.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this assertion validates and why it matters

element_description.description: Semantic description of the element to check (for element_exists, element_text, element_not_exists, element_count, pattern_match, value_matches_variable, text_changed)

expected_value.description: Expected text value for element_text assertions

pattern.description: Regex pattern to match against element text for pattern_match assertions. Also used as a format hint in value_matches_variable assertions.

variable_name.description: Variable name (declared in 'variables' block) whose captured value to compare against for value_matches_variable assertions

expected_count.description: Expected element count for element_count assertions

expected_visual_state.description: Expected visual state for visual_state assertions

reference_screenshot.description: Relative path to the reference baseline screenshot for screenshot_match assertions

tolerance.description: Similarity threshold for screenshot_match assertions (0.0 = any match, 1.0 = pixel-perfect)
